volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Iphigenia among the Taurians (Section 610 - Section 742)

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: ῥίζης πέφυκας τοῖς φίλοις τʼ ὀρθῶς φίλος .
sections.translation: stock , and are rightly a friend to your friends ! May that one of my relatives who is left be such as you ! For I am not without a brother , strangers , except in so far as I do not see him .

sections.label: Section 614
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δὲ βούλῃ ταῦτα , τόνδε πέμψομεν
sections.translation: Since you wish it , I will send this man

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: δέλτον φέροντα , σὺ δὲ θανῇ · πολλὴ δέ τις
sections.translation: with the tablet , and you will die ; a great eagerness for this seems to possess you .

sections.label: Section 617
sections.original: θύσει δὲ τίς με καὶ τὰ δεινὰ τλήσεται ;
sections.translation: Who will sacrifice me and dare such a dreadful deed ?

sections.label: Section 618
sections.original: ἐγώ · θεᾶς γὰρ τῆσδε προστροπὴν ἔχω .
sections.translation: I will ; for I hold the office of this goddess .

sections.label: Section 619
sections.original: ἄζηλά γʼ , ὦ νεᾶνι , κοὐκ εὐδαίμονα .
sections.translation: It is not envied , lady , and not blessed .

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰς ἀνάγκην κείμεθʼ , ἣν φυλακτέον .
sections.translation: But I am dedicated to necessity , which must be kept .

sections.label: Section 621
sections.original: αὐτὴ ξίφει θύουσα θῆλυς ἄρσενας ;
sections.translation: Do you yourself , a woman , sacrifice men with the sword ?

sections.label: Section 622
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλὰ χαίτην ἀμφὶ σὴν χερνίψομαι .
sections.translation: No ; but I sprinkle the holy water around your hair .

sections.label: Section 623
sections.original: ὁ δὲ σφαγεὺς τίς ; εἰ τάδʼ ἱστορεῖν με χρή .
sections.translation: Who is the slayer ? If I may ask this .

sections.label: Section 624
sections.original: ἔσω δόμων τῶνδʼ εἰσὶν οἷς μέλει τάδε .
sections.translation: That charge belongs to those within this temple .

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: τάφος δὲ ποῖος δέξεταί μʼ , ὅταν θάνω ;
sections.translation: What sort of tomb will receive me , when I die ?

sections.label: Section 626
sections.original: πῦρ ἱερὸν ἔνδον χάσμα τʼ εὐρωπὸν πέτρας .
sections.translation: The sacred fire within and the wide hollow of a cave .

sections.label: Section 627
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! Would that my sister ’ s hand might lay out my body !

sections.label: Section 628
sections.original: μάταιον εὐχήν , ὦ τάλας , ὅστις ποτʼ εἶ ,
sections.translation: You have prayed in vain , unhappy youth , whoever you are ; for she lives far from a barbarian land .

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: οὐ μήν , ἐπειδὴ τυγχάνεις Ἀργεῖος ὤν ,
sections.translation: Yet indeed , since you happen to be an Argive , I too will not leave out any favor that I can do . I will set much ornament on the tomb and quench your body with yellow oil , and throw onto your funeral pyre the gleaming honey , that streams from flowers ,

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: ξουθῆς μελίσσης ἐς πυρὰν βαλῶ σέθεν .
sections.translation: of the tawny mountain bee .

sections.label: Section 636
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἶμι δέλτον τʼ ἐκ θεᾶς ἀνακτόρων
sections.translation: But I will go and bring the tablet from the temple of the goddess ; take care not to bear me ill-will .

sections.label: Section 638
sections.original: φυλάσσετʼ αὐτούς , πρόσπολοι , δεσμῶν ἄτερ —
sections.translation: Guard them , attendants , without chains . Perhaps I will send unexpected news to one of my friends ,

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: πέμψω πρὸς Ἄργος , ὃν μάλιστʼ ἐγὼ φιλῶ ,
sections.translation: whom I especially love , in Argos ; and the tablet , in telling him that those whom he thought dead are alive , will report a joy that can be believed . Exit Iphigenia .

sections.label: Section 644
sections.original: κατολοφύρομαι σὲ τὸν χερνίβων
sections.translation: ( to Orestes .) I raise a lament for you ; the drops from the holy water ,

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: ῥανίσι μελόμενον αἱμακταῖς .
sections.translation: mingled with blood , will soon take you into their care .

sections.label: Section 646
sections.original: οἶκτος γὰρ οὐ ταῦτʼ , ἀλλὰ χαίρετʼ , ὦ ξέναι .
sections.translation: This is not a case for pity , but farewell , strangers .

sections.label: Section 647
sections.original: σὲ δὲ τύχας μάκαρος , ὦ
sections.translation: ( to Pylades .) We honor you , young man , for your happy fate , because you will tread on your native land some day .

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: ἄζηλά τοι φίλοισι , θνῃσκόντων φίλων .
sections.translation: An unenviable fate indeed for a friend , when his friend is to die .

sections.label: Section 651
sections.original: ὦ σχέτλιοι πομπαί .
sections.translation: O cruel mission !

sections.label: Section 652
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ , διόλλυσαι .
sections.translation: Ah , ah ! You are destroyed ! Alas , alas ! Which is better ?

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: ἔτι γὰρ ἀμφίλογα δίδυμα μέμονε φρήν ,
sections.translation: For still my mind disputes a double argument , shall I mourn for you or rather for you .

sections.label: Section 658
sections.original: Πυλάδη , πέπονθας ταὐτὸ πρὸς θεῶν ἐμοί ;
sections.translation: By the gods , Pylades , do you feel the same thing I do ?

sections.label: Section 659
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ · ἐρωτᾷς οὐ λέγειν ἔχοντά με .
sections.translation: I do not know ; I have no reply to your question .

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: τίς ἐστὶν ἡ νεᾶνις ; ὡς Ἑλληνικῶς
sections.translation: Who is the girl ? How like a Hellene she questioned me about the labors in Ilium and the return of the Achaeans , and Calchas , wise in omens , and Achilles ’ name ; and how she pitied the wretched Agamemnon , and asked me about

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: γυναῖκα παῖδάς τε . ἔστιν ἡ ξένη γένος
sections.translation: his wife and children ! This stranger is an Argive by race , and from that land ; or she would not be sending the tablet and examining these things , as if she had some share in Argos ’ prosperity .

sections.label: Section 669
sections.original: ἔφθης με μικρόν · ταὐτὰ δὲ φθάσας λέγεις ,
sections.translation: You are not much ahead of me : I was about to say the same things you said ,

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: πλὴν ἕν · τὰ γὰρ τῶν βασιλέων παθήματα
sections.translation: except this : all who move about in the world know what happens to kings . But I have arrived at another consideration .

sections.label: Section 673
sections.original: τίνʼ ; ἐς τὸ κοινὸν δοὺς ἄμεινον ἂν μάθοις .
sections.translation: What is it ? Share it with me so that you may know better .

sections.label: Section 674
sections.original: αἰσχρὸν θανόντος σοῦ βλέπειν ἡμᾶς φάος ·
sections.translation: It is shameful for me to live when you are dead ;

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: κοινῇ τʼ ἔπλευσα ⋯ δεῖ με καὶ κοινῇ θανεῖν .
sections.translation: I sailed together with you , and I ought to die together with you . For I will seem a coward and base in Argos and Phocis of the many mountain folds . Most will think — for most people are base — that I betrayed you and saved myself to come home alone ,

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: ἢ καὶ φονεύσας ἐπὶ νοσοῦσι δώμασι
sections.translation: or I plotted your death , in the afflictions of your house , for the sake of your kingdom , since I married your sister and heiress . I fear these things and I am ashamed ; and I must breathe my last with you ,

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: καὶ σὺν σφαγῆναι καὶ πυρωθῆναι δέμας ,
sections.translation: be slaughtered with you and consumed on the pyre ; because I am your friend and I fear reproach .

sections.label: Section 687
sections.original: εὔφημα φώνει · τἀμὰ δεῖ φέρειν κακά ,
sections.translation: Hush ! I must bear my own ills , and when the grief is single , I will not bear it double . What you call vile and infamous ,

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: ταὔτʼ ἔστιν ἡμῖν , εἴ σε συμμοχθοῦντʼ ἐμοὶ
sections.translation: would be mine , if I cause you , who have toiled with me , to die ; for it is not a hardship for me , suffering as I do at the hands of the gods , to give up my life . But you are prosperous , and you have a house that is pure , not afflicted , while mine is impious and unfortunate .

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: σωθεὶς δέ , παῖδας ἐξ ἐμῆς ὁμοσπόρου
sections.translation: If you are saved and get sons from my sister , whom I gave to you for wife , my name would remain and the whole house of my father would not be wiped out in childlessness . But go , and live , and dwell in my father ’ s house .

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: ὅταν δʼ ἐς Ἑλλάδʼ ἵππιόν τʼ Ἄργος μόλῃς ,
sections.translation: And when you come to Hellas and to Argos of the horses , I charge you , by this right hand : heap up a tomb and build a memorial for me , and let my sister give her hair and tears to the tomb . Report that I died at the hand of an Argive

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: γυναικός , ἀμφὶ βωμὸν ἁγνισθεὶς φόνῳ .
sections.translation: woman , at an altar , purified for death . Do not ever betray my sister , when you see how lonely is my father ’ s house that you have joined by marriage . And now farewell ; I have found you the dearest of my friends , you who have hunted with me , grown up with me ,

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original: ὦ πόλλʼ ἐνεγκὼν τῶν ἐμῶν ἄχθη κακῶν .
sections.translation: and borne with me many miseries . Phoebus , though a prophet , has deceived me ; creating his plot , he drove me far away from Hellas , ashamed of his earlier prophecies . I gave him my all and trusted in his words ,

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: μητέρα κατακτὰς αὐτὸς ἀνταπόλλυμαι .
sections.translation: killed my mother , and myself perish in turn .

sections.label: Section 716
sections.original: ἔσται τάφος σοι , καὶ κασιγνήτης λέχος
sections.translation: You will have a tomb , and I will never betray your sister ’ s bed , unhappy youth , since I will hold you dearer when dead than when alive . But the prophecy of the god has not destroyed you yet ;

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: μάντευμα · καίτοι γʼ ἐγγὺς ἕστηκας φόνου .
sections.translation: although you stand near to slaughter . Great misfortune can offer great reversals , when it is fated ; it can indeed .

sections.label: Section 723
sections.original: σίγα · τὰ Φοίβου δʼ οὐδὲν ὠφελεῖ μʼ ἔπη ·
sections.translation: Silence ; the words of Phoebus are no benefit to me ; here comes the woman out of the temple .

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: ἀπέλθεθʼ ὑμεῖς καὶ παρευτρεπίζετε
sections.translation: ( to the guard .) Go away and make the preparations within for those who attend to the sacrifice .

sections.label: Section 727
sections.original: δέλτου μὲν αἵδε πολύθυροι διαπτυχαί ,
sections.translation: Here are the many folds of the tablet , strangers . Hear what I want in addition . No man is the same when he is in troubles

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: ὅταν τε πρὸς τὸ θάρσος ἐκ φόβου πέσῃ .
sections.translation: and when he falls out of fear into courage . I am afraid that when the one who is going to take this tablet to Argos leaves this land , he will put aside my letter as worth nothing .

sections.label: Section 734
sections.original: τί δῆτα βούλῃ ; τίνος ἀμηχανεῖς πέρι ;
sections.translation: What do you want , then ? What are you perplexed about ?

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: ὅρκον δότω μοι τάσδε πορθμεύσειν γραφὰς
sections.translation: Let him give me an oath that he will convey this letter to Argos , to the friends to whom I wish to send it .

sections.label: Section 737
sections.original: ἦ κἀντιδώσεις τῷδε τοὺς αὐτοὺς λόγους ;
sections.translation: And will you give in return the same words to him ?

sections.label: Section 738
sections.original: τί χρῆμα δράσειν ἢ τί μὴ δράσειν ; λέγε .
sections.translation: To do what , or refrain from doing ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 739
sections.original: ἐκ γῆς ἀφήσειν μὴ θανόντα βαρβάρου .
sections.translation: To send him from this barbarous land alive .

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: δίκαιον εἶπας · πῶς γὰρ ἀγγείλειεν ἄν ;
sections.translation: What you say is right ; how else could he deliver it ?

sections.label: Section 741
sections.original: ἦ καὶ τύραννος ταῦτα συγχωρήσεται ;
sections.translation: Will the king agree to this ?

sections.label: Section 742
sections.original: ναί .
sections.translation: Yes . I will persuade the king , and I myself will put this man on the ship .
